meta.purpose: Per-country MCI (mild cognitive impairment) + SCD (subjective cognitive decline) prevalence sources for the dementia-exposome map. Feeds build_mci_national() and a future build_scd_national().
meta.method: Cloud deep-research: 66-agent fan-out (Tier A per-country for ~58 data-rich countries + Tier B one-agent-per-World-Bank-region for the long tail), WebSearch-only, values extracted from abstracts / open-access full text. Per docs/side-projects/dementia-exposome/cloud-research-brief-mci-scd.md. Seeded from scripts/provenance-audit-cognitive.json.
meta.caveats: MODELLED / HETEROGENEOUS — indicative only, do not compare across countries. MCI: MoCA-only overestimates (~20%+), MMSE underestimates, CIND is broader than Petersen/DSM-5 MCI — the tool used is recorded in `criteria`. SCD: single-item self-report inflates to 40-60% (`criteria: self-report-single-item`, `confidence: low`); SCD-I structured framework is rare. `access: paywalled(...)` values were read from the abstract and should be verified at fetch. GBD does NOT produce MCI/SCD.
meta.regionalBaselineMCI: Bai et al. 2022, Age & Ageing (WB-region pooled, 50+) — already in build_mci_national(); countries without a national value here fall back to it.

regionalNotesSCD_MCI.East Asia & Pacific: Best regional MCI pooled anchor: Southeast Asia MCI ~17.08% in older adults, from "Emerging trends in cognitive impairment and dementia among older populations in Asia: a systematic review," Journal of Global Health 2024;14:04233 (https://jogh.org/2024/jogh-14-04233; PMC11544519) — but SE Asia is severely data-sparse (only 2 of 39 included studies were Southeast Asian). The SEACURE consensus (2025, Southeast Asia) explicitly states there is no official regional MCI estimate. No SCD-specific regional meta-analysis covers these countries. Pacific islands (Fiji, Papua New Guinea, Solomon Islands, Vanuatu, New Caledonia), Timor-Leste, Cambodia, and North Korea have NO country-specific MCI or SCD prevalence; Western Pacific dementia data is sparse and heterogeneous (Lancet Regional Health – Western Pacific 2024, "Risk factors for the neurodegenerative dementias in the Western Pacific region," PIIS2666-6065(24)00045-2; PMC11471060). SCD prevalence is absent across ALL listed countries. Country records below (Laos, Myanmar, Brunei, Mongolia) use screening-tool "cognitive impairment" figures (HDS-R / Mini-COG / local scales) as MCI proxies — none are DSM-5/Petersen MCI, so all are confidence=low and likely overestimate true MCI while conflating dementia-range cases.
regionalNotesSCD_MCI.Central Asia & Caucasus: No dedicated MCI/SCD meta-analysis exists for Central Asia & the Caucasus; the region is largely uncovered by dementia epidemiology. The only genuinely multi-country regional data is the preliminary Kazakh–Kyrgyz comparison (Int Psychogeriatrics 2020, abstract 338, https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/international-psychogeriatrics/article/338-risk-factors-for-moderate-cognitive-impairment-and-dementia-in-the-kazakh-and-kyrgyz-populations-preliminary-results/FDA10846FCD955E4AD9F9029368E6A1F). For pooled fallback use the global community-dwelling estimate: Bai W, et al. Worldwide prevalence of MCI among community dwellers aged 50+: meta-analysis. Age Ageing 2022;51(8):afac173 — pooled MCI 15.56% (https://academic.oup.com/ageing/article/51/8/afac173/6670563). A nursing-home MCI meta-analysis grouped 'Europe & Central Asia' at ~19.7% (institutional) vs ~10.9% community (Transl Psychiatry 2023, https://www.nature.com/articles/s41398-023-02361-1). No SCD prevalence data was located for any country in this region. Azerbaijan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan and Uzbekistan have no usable country-specific MCI/SCD prevalence and fall back to the regional/global pooled estimate.
regionalNotesSCD_MCI.Europe (tail): Primary multi-country source: SHARE (Survey of Health, Ageing and Retirement in Europe), 2022 wave — harmonized MCI + dementia estimates for 27 European countries + Israel (n=47,773, age 65+), validated against HCAP. MCI averaged ~24% (range 17.2% Sweden to 31.1% Portugal); highest ~30% ("almost a third") in Bulgaria, Cyprus, Greece, Portugal; lowest ~17% in Austria/Germany/Sweden/Denmark/Switzerland. This is the fallback pooled source for the in-scope SHARE countries lacking a national study — Estonia (ee), Croatia (hr), Lithuania (lt), Latvia (lv), Slovenia (si), Slovakia (sk): their exact per-country MCI values are in the paper's supplement (not in the abstract — need full text) and sit near the 24% mean. TRAP: SHARE MCI is screening-derived (SHARE-Cog 3-item) and likely OVERESTIMATES true clinical (Petersen) MCI. Citation: Bacher/Börsch-Supan et al., "Harmonized prevalence estimates of dementia in Europe vary strongly with childhood education," Scientific Reports 2025, https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-97691-z (PMID 40269049; PMC12019132). Global MCI triangulation for fallback: pooled 19.7% (Frontiers Aging Neurosci 2023, PMC10641463) and 23.7% (BMC Geriatrics 2025, PMID 40329163). NO usable MCI/SCD prevalence found for Bosnia-Herzegovina (ba), Belarus (by), Moldova (md), Montenegro (me), North Macedonia (mk), Kosovo (xk), Serbia (rs), or Greenland (gl): Serbia has only self-reported memory/concentration difficulty (2019 national health survey, PMC11266187 — not diagnosed MCI); Greenland has a dementia register (254 cases, 2011) + RUDAS/Mini-Cog validation (PMC8245097) but no MCI/SCD rate; the others appear only in Alzheimer Europe / CEE dementia (not MCI) meta-analyses. No multi-country structured SCD-I estimate exists for the region — only Luxembourg MemoVie complaints (26%).
regionalNotesSCD_MCI.Latin America & Caribbean (tail): Regional pooled MCI estimate (use as fallback for countries with no usable specific value): "The prevalence of mild cognitive impairment in Latin America and the Caribbean: a systematic review and meta-analysis", Aging & Mental Health 2022;26(9), DOI 10.1080/13607863.2021.2003297 (OA, PMC9466284). 11 studies, n=20,220, 9 countries (Brazil, Mexico, Argentina, Colombia, Peru, Cuba, Dominican Republic, Venezuela, Costa Rica). All-type MCI pooled range 6.8–25.5%; amnestic MCI 3.1–10.5%; prevalence rises with age and falls with education. Second useful multi-country source: 10/66 Dementia Research Group (Sosa 2012, PLOS Med, DOI 10.1371/journal.pmed.1001170) — strict Mayo amnestic MCI (aMCI) across Cuba/DR/Venezuela/Puerto Rico/Peru/Mexico: crude 0.8–4.3%, standardized 0.6–4.6% (underestimates all-type MCI). No comparable LAC-wide SCD meta-analysis exists; SCD figures are single-country and self-report-inflated (40–60% range), so treat with low confidence.
regionalNotesSCD_MCI.Middle East & North Africa (tail): MCI/SCD-specific regional pooled data for the MENA tail are sparse (GBD does NOT produce MCI/SCD). Closest MCI-specific regional meta-analysis: Asnakew et al., "Magnitude and predictors of mild cognitive impairment among older populations in Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis," Translational Psychiatry 2025 (36 studies, 2005-2023) — pooled MCI prevalence ~26.78% for 2023 among older Africans; covers the North-African tail (Sudan, Algeria, Libya, Tunisia). https://www.nature.com/articles/s41398-025-03620-z (DOI 10.1038/s41398-025-03620-z). For dementia triangulation across the whole MENA set (dementia, NOT MCI): Sedighi et al., "Prevalence of dementia in selected Middle East and North Africa (MENA) countries: A systematic review and meta-analysis," Alzheimer's & Dementia Jan 2026 — pooled dementia 12.16% (95% CI 9.61-14.96), 52 studies / 1,045,908 people (Israel 17.0%, Iran 13.2%, Turkey 11.4%, Saudi 8.34%, Egypt 6.86%). https://alz-journals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/alz.71109 (DOI 10.1002/alz.71109). No MENA-wide SCD meta-analysis identified; single-item subjective-memory measures in the region (Lebanon LSAHA 56.3%; Türkiye 61%) inflate SCD and are low-confidence. Country records for ly=Libya, tn=Tunisia, om=Oman, qa=Qatar, sy=Syria are omitted — no usable country-specific community MCI/SCD prevalence found (only hospital dementia case series or MoCA-norm/validation samples); these fall back to the regional pooled estimate.
regionalNotesSCD_MCI.West & Central Africa: Best regional pooled estimate for fallback: "Cognitive Ageing in Africa: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Pre-Dementia Prevalence and Associated Factors" (EA Health Research Journal, 2025) — 28 studies, 9 African countries, n=26,067; pooled MCI 20% (95% CI 14-28%, n=23,749) and SCD 50% (95% CI 40-59%, n=2,318). Extreme heterogeneity (I²≈99%): studies using neuropsychological cut-offs alone reported ~3x higher MCI than those applying clinical criteria — key methodological caveat for this region. URL: https://eahrj.eahealth.org/eah/article/view/840 . Corroborating: "Magnitude and predictors of mild cognitive impairment among older populations in Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis" (Translational Psychiatry, Oct 2025) pooled Africa MCI ~26.4% (95% CI 15.4-41.6%), https://www.nature.com/articles/s41398-025-03620-z . Francophone context: "Dementia and cognitive impairment in French-speaking Sub-Saharan Africa: a comprehensive review" (2023) reports population cognitive-impairment prevalence ranging 10.4% (rural Benin) to 37.9% (CAR survey); DSM-IV dementia 6.4%, 10/66 dementia 21.6% — https://scholarworks.utrgv.edu/som_pub/1078/ . NOTE: No country-specific SCD prevalence exists for any country in this region — all SCD falls back to the regional pooled ~50%. GBD does not produce MCI/SCD.
regionalNotesSCD_MCI.East & Southern Africa: Regional pooled fallback: East Africa scoping review of population-based studies (Yenesew MA et al., J Alzheimers Dis 2024;100(4)) reports cognitive impairment 7–44% and dementia 6–23% among adults aged ≥50–70 — but only 3 CI + 4 dementia population studies exist for all of East Africa, so data are extremely scarce. URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11380225/ (OA). Triangulate with: (a) Africa-wide MCI systematic review & meta-analysis (Translational Psychiatry 2025, doi s41398-025-03620-z, https://www.nature.com/articles/s41398-025-03620-z) — exact pooled % not captured in abstract, need full text; (b) LMIC MCI systematic review (McGrattan AM et al., J Alzheimers Dis 2021;79(2):743-762, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33361599/) which incorporates the Tanzania Hai data. SCD: no structured SCD-I prevalence exists for ANY country in this region; the only self-report figures (Malawi 66%, Botswana census 22%) are single-item / disability-construct measures and are inflated/mismatched — low confidence. For the ~13 countries with nothing (Angola, Burundi, Djibouti, Eritrea, Madagascar, Mozambique, Namibia, Rwanda, Somalia, South Sudan, Eswatini, Zimbabwe, Lesotho) use the regional pooled estimate.
regionalNotesSCD_MCI.South Asia (tail): No country-specific MCI or SCD prevalence exists for Afghanistan or Bhutan; both should fall back to a South Asia regional pooled MCI estimate. Best regional coverage: Islam MR et al., "Emerging trends in cognitive impairment and dementia among older populations in Asia: A systematic review," J Glob Health 2024;14:04233 (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC11544519/) — of 39 eligible 2019-23 studies, only 7 were from South Asia and 0 from Afghanistan/Bhutan, confirming a data void; it reports cognitive-impairment/dementia trends, not a South-Asia pooled MCI point estimate. Global pooled MCI ~19.7% for triangulation: Bai W et al., Front Aging Neurosci 2023;15:1227112 (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10641463/). Nearest South-Asian MCI point estimate is a 2023 Delhi community study (~9.35%, HMSE+MoCA, India). AFGHANISTAN — the ONLY attributable cognitive data is a DEMENTIA study, not MCI/SCD: Trani JF et al., "Multidimensional poverty is associated with dementia among adults in Afghanistan," eClinicalMedicine 2023;58:101906, DOI 10.1016/j.eclinm.2023.101906 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36969341/); cross-sectional survey, n=478 adults aged 50+, Badakhshan & Ghazni provinces (Feb-Apr 2022), CIDAPSA project, using the Rowland Universal Dementia Assessment Scale (RUDAS). It stages dementia (mild vs moderate-to-severe) and does not report an MCI or SCD prevalence; RUDAS over-identifies impairment in low-literacy populations, so its figures are not usable as MCI. A separate mental-health survey cited ~5.8% dementia among Afghans 65+ (dementia, not MCI). BHUTAN — no MCI, SCD, or dementia prevalence study located (neighbouring Nepal ~8.8-10.7% dementia and Bangladesh data exist, but nothing for Bhutan); rely on regional pooled fallback.

mci.criteria: MoCA cutoff <26, score-band classification (mild band); NOT Petersen/DSM-5 — MoCA-only OVERESTIMATES MCI, and 69.3% fell below cutoff which is implausibly high for true MCI
mci.citation: Cognitive decline and quality of life: evidence from community-dwelling older adults in the United Arab Emirates. Frontiers in Aging, 2026. Cross-sectional, n=606 community-dwelling older adults (Dubai/Sharjah), 55.4% male, 84.2% Emirati. MoCA<26 in 69.3%; classified as mild 40.9%, moderate 24.8%, severe 3.6%. This 40.9% is a MoCA score band, not a validated Petersen MCI diagnosis, so it substantially inflates true MCI prevalence.
mci.subnational: emirate level; sample drawn from 2 emirates (Dubai, Sharjah) — not national coverage of all 7 emirates
mci.representative: community (community-dwelling older adults in Dubai/Sharjah; convenience/community sample, not nationally representative)

mci.criteria: MMSE only (defined MCI as MMSE 19-22 band) — nonstandard cutoff; MMSE underestimates MCI and this band is atypically low
mci.citation: Estimating the Prevalence of Cognitive Impairment and Its Associated Factors in Albania: A Nationwide Cross-Sectional Study. Brain Sci 2024;14(10):955. Door-to-door neuropsychological screening, 12 Albanian districts, residents >50. Overall cognitive impairment/early dementia 14.04%; MCI band (MMSE 19-22) 5.51%.
mci.subnational: 12 districts sampled (nationwide coverage)
mci.representative: national (door-to-door, 12 districts, age 50+)

mci.criteria: MoCA single-cutoff screen, no clinical confirmation — reported as broad 'cognitive impairment' (CI), MoCA-only OVERESTIMATES MCI
mci.citation: Jivalagian et al. 2025, 'Understanding Cognitive Decline in Armenia's Aging Population: A Nationwide Screening Study', Alzheimer's & Dementia (conference abstract); NGO (Alzheimer's Care Armenia) screening program across 8 provinces
mci.subnational: 8 of 11 provinces (marzer) screened; age-strata breakdown reported
mci.representative: national (nationwide NGO screening program, convenience sample — not random/population-based)

mci.criteria: MMSE-based CIND (cognitive impairment, no dementia) used as MCI proxy — not a full Petersen neuropsych battery; MMSE tends to UNDERESTIMATE MCI but CIND is a broader construct than MCI, and the sample is socially vulnerable/low-education (inflates cognitive impairment). Door-to-door population survey.
mci.citation: Serrano CM, Sorbara M, Minond A, et al. A population-based study of cognitive impairment in socially vulnerable adults in Argentina. The Matanza Riachuelo Study — Preliminary Results. Dement Neuropsychol (2013). Door-to-door survey, adults >60, Matanza-Riachuelo Basin (suburban Buenos Aires); 2437 assessed, 73.6% met inclusion (~1793 analyzed); MMSE-based. Prevalence: CIND (MCI proxy) 18.1%, dementia 8.3%, total cognitive impairment 26.4%. TRIANGULATION: LAC pooled all-type MCI 14.95% (95% CI 6.81-25.52%), Ribeiro et al., Aging Ment Health 2022 (PMC9466284); also Neuquen high-social-risk >60 study (PubMed 30778407). No Argentine national registry/cohort MCI figure exists.
mci.subnational: 1 region — Matanza-Riachuelo Basin (ACUMAR), suburban Buenos Aires; not national
mci.representative: community (regional, socially vulnerable/low-income population; NOT nationally representative)

scd.criteria: structured-questionnaire (subjective memory complaints, SMC) — NOT a single self-report item (would inflate to 40-60%) and NOT the full SCD-I framework; 602/3285 = 18.3%. Pooled multi-country LAC figure, not Argentina-specific.
scd.citation: Caldichoury N, Morales-Asencio B, et al. Subjective memory complaints in Latin American older adults: Prevalence and risk factors. J Alzheimers Dis (2025). Cross-sectional online survey during COVID-19 lockdown (Jun-Oct 2020), n=3285 older adults from 10 LAC countries INCLUDING Argentina; SMC present in 602 (~18.3%). NOT Argentina-specific and an online convenience sample — no Argentina-only SCD/SMC prevalence located. PMC12775176.
scd.representative: multi-country LAC pooled (incl. Argentina), online convenience sample during COVID-19 lockdown — NOT nationally representative

mci.criteria: Petersen-style neuropsychological 1.5 SD-below-norm paradigm, MCI without dementia (NOT MoCA/MMSE-only). One-or-more-domain impairment. Single-age-75 cohort inflates estimate vs general 65+ population.
mci.citation: VITA (Vienna Transdanube Aging) study — 'VITA: Subtypes of mild cognitive impairment in a community-based cohort at the age of 75 years', Int J Geriatr Psychiatry (2005). MCI 23.8% (95% CI 20.4–27.5) of the full age-75 cohort (141/592) by 1.5 SD paradigm; dementia 2.5%. Community-based single-birth-cohort in Vienna's Transdanube district. Verified numeric value extracted from abstract; exact author/volume not confirmed from abstract (need full text).
mci.representative: regional (single Vienna district / Transdanube; single birth cohort aged 75) — not nationally representative; best available Austrian population-based MCI figure

mci.criteria: Comprehensive neuropsychological battery with Winblad/Petersen consensus MCI criteria (impairment ~1.5 SD below norms, preserved function); NOT MoCA-only. Note: 34.8% is elevated, driven by the old 70-90 age band plus sensitive multi-domain criteria (amnestic 19.3% + non-amnestic 15.5%; range 30.9% at 70-75 to 39.1% at 85-90).
mci.citation: Sachdev PS, Lipnicki DM, Crawford J, et al. Mild cognitive impairment in a community sample: The Sydney Memory and Ageing Study (MAS). Alzheimer's & Dementia. 2012. MCI prevalence 34.8% (amnestic 19.3%, non-amnestic 15.5%) in community-dwelling adults aged 70-90; increases with age (30.9% at 70-75 to 39.1% at 85-90). Australia's flagship ageing cohort but geographically Sydney-based. Paywalled (Elsevier) - need full text for exact 95% CI.
mci.representative: community (2 areas of Sydney, non-institutionalised electoral-roll sample); treated as AU reference ageing cohort but not nationally sampled
mci.n: 1037 baseline non-demented cohort (~873 in MCI analysis)

scd.criteria: SCD ascertained via harmonized structured measures across cohorts (qualitative harmonization + Item Response Theory) - stronger than single-item self-report, avoiding the 40-60% inflation seen with single questions. Not the formal SCD-I framework but a rigorous cross-cohort harmonization.
scd.citation: Rohr S, Pabst A, Riedel-Heller SG, et al. Estimating prevalence of subjective cognitive decline in and across international cohort studies of aging: a COSMIC study. Alzheimer's Research & Therapy. 2020. Pooled SCD prevalence 23.8% (qualitative harmonization) / 25.6% (IRT) among 39,387 cognitively unimpaired adults 60+ across 16 cohorts from 15 countries, INCLUDING Australian cohorts (AIBL, PATH, Sydney MAS). International pooled estimate, not Australia-specific; SCD higher in men, lower education, LMICs.
scd.representative: international pooled IPD (16 cohorts/15 countries) incl. Australian cohorts AIBL, PATH & Sydney MAS as a subset; not an AU-specific national figure
scd.n: 39,387 cognitively unimpaired adults 60+ (pooled; Australian cohorts a subset)

mci.criteria: MMSE (Bengali-adapted), cutoff-based; reported as 'mild/moderate cognitive impairment' (21.6%); overall CI incl. severe = 27.9%. Not Petersen/DSM-5 MCI — MMSE underestimates true MCI and mild+moderate categories combined
mci.citation: Prevalence and predictors of cognitive impairment among the elderly in Bangladesh. Journal of Public Health (2021). Cross-sectional survey of older adults (>=60y); MMSE administered to n=111 analysis subset: 21.6% mild/moderate CI (women 25.5%, men 18.3%), 6.3% severe, 27.9% any CI. Paywalled — figures from abstract; need full text for CI and sampling detail
mci.representative: community; small MMSE analysis subset (n=111) drawn from a 1015-person cross-sectional survey — not nationally representative

mci.criteria: Leganes cognitive test (cognitive impairment, not Petersen MCI)
mci.citation: Onadja Y, Atchessi N, Soura BA, Rossier C, Zunzunegui MV. Gender differences in cognitive impairment and mobility disability in old age: a cross-sectional study in Ouagadougou, Burkina Faso. Arch Gerontol Geriatr. 2013. n=981 aged 50+ (Ouagadougou Health & Demographic Surveillance System, 2010). Cognitive impairment (Leganes test) 27.6% in women vs 7.7% in men; no combined point prevalence published. PMID 23827740.
mci.representative: regional (urban Ouagadougou HDSS, aged 50+)

mci.criteria: Clinical MCI diagnosis (Petersen-style) after two-stage screen + full neuropsych/imaging; regional urban (Varna)
mci.citation: Prevalence of Dementia and Mild Cognitive Impairment in a Bulgarian Urban Population (Varna). Am J Alzheimers Dis Other Demen (~2012). 605 sampled age 65+, 540 screened; MCI diagnosed clinically in 36 = 6.7%. Triangulation: SHARE 2022 national MCI ~30% (Sci Rep 2025) but that is screening-based and likely an overestimate; this clinical regional value is the higher-quality MCI criterion.
mci.subnational: single city (Varna)
mci.representative: regional/urban (Varna city, age 65+)

mci.criteria: CSI-D + Five-Word Test (cognitive impairment proxy, not strict Petersen MCI)
mci.citation: Guerchet M, et al. Cognitive impairment and dementia in elderly people living in rural Benin, west Africa. Dement Geriatr Cogn Disord. 2009;27(1):34-42. Door-to-door survey, aged >=65, rural (Djidja area). Cognitive impairment prevalence 10.4%, dementia 2.6%. Age, depressive disorder and absence of APOE e2 associated. PMID 19136831; DOI 10.1159/000188661.
mci.representative: community (rural Djidja, aged 65+)

mci.criteria: Mini-COG; 'possible/suspected cognitive impairment' screen — NOT DSM-5 MCI (Mini-COG favours dementia-range detection); convenience sample; tool recorded
mci.citation: Community-Based Dementia Screening Initiative in Brunei: Final Results (Demensia Brunei; Teo et al.). J Gerontol Geriatr (MDPI). 2026;74(2):8. n=1962 screened at senior centres, health centres, marketplaces, RIPAS Hospital, government departments; 14.5% possible cognitive impairment on Mini-COG. Need full text to confirm DOI.
mci.subnational: nationwide multiple venues (convenience), not random-sampled by district
mci.representative: community convenience sample across Brunei; not a population-representative survey

mci.criteria: MCI operationalized via adapted Modified MMSE (3MS) + informant interview + longitudinal cognitive testing + brain CT; age-standardized. Indigenous Amazonian forager-horticulturalists (dementia among lowest worldwide, 1.2%). Petersen-style clinical adjudication, not MoCA/MMSE-cutoff.
mci.citation: Gatz M et al. Prevalence of dementia and mild cognitive impairment in indigenous Bolivian forager-horticulturalists. Alzheimer's & Dementia. 2023;19(1):44-55.
mci.subnational: single ethnic-population catchment (Beni dept, lowland Bolivia)
mci.representative: community/indigenous — Tsimane & Moseten only; NOT nationally representative

mci.criteria: CIND (cognitive impairment no dementia) via clinical/neuropsychological consensus diagnosis, single-phase cross-sectional survey; CIND contains the MCI concept but is NOT strict Petersen MCI (broader than MCI)
mci.citation: César KG, Brucki SMD, Takada LT, ... Nitrini R, et al. Prevalence of Cognitive Impairment Without Dementia and Dementia in Tremembé, Brazil. Alzheimer Dis Assoc Disord. 2016;30(3):264-271. CIND 19.5%, dementia 17.5%; n=630 aged 60+ (mean age 71.3, mean education 4.9 yrs); influenced by age and education, no significant sex difference. Lippincott DOI likely 10.1097/WAD.0000000000000122 (not fetch-verified) — PubMed URL confirmed.
mci.representative: community/regional — single municipality (Tremembé, São Paulo state); door-to-door population sample, not nationally representative

scd.criteria: self-report (population-based, self-reported cognitive/memory decline in ELSI-Brazil); NOT the SCD-I structured framework — but a moderate estimate (below the 40-60% single-item inflation band)
scd.citation: Borelli WV, ... Castilhos RM, et al. Subjective cognitive decline in Brazil: Prevalence and association with dementia modifiable risk factors in a population-based study. Alzheimers Dement (Amst). 2022;14(1):e12368. SCD prevalence 29.21% (95% CI 28.22–30.21), varying by region, sex and age; n=8,138 from ELSI-Brazil (Brazilian Longitudinal Study of Aging). First SCD prevalence study in Brazil/Latin America; higher than high-income countries. DOI 10.1002/dad2.12368.
scd.subnational: 5 macro-regions (national coverage; SCD prevalence reported to vary by region)
scd.representative: national — ELSI-Brazil population-based cohort, adults aged 50+

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item — Washington Group Short Set 'difficulty remembering or concentrating' (a functional-disability measure, NOT clinical SCD/SCD-I; construct mismatch). Rose from 6.4% (2017 Demographic Survey) to 22.2% (2022 Census).
scd.citation: Cognitive disability among older adults in Botswana: prevalence, trends, and sociodemographic determinants from cross-sectional data (2025). Data: 2022 Botswana Population & Housing Census + 2017 Botswana Demographic Survey; Washington Group Short Set.
scd.representative: national (census self-report; disability construct, not MCI/SCD — interpret cautiously)

mci.criteria: CIND (cognitive impairment, no dementia) via 3MS + clinical exam; broader than Petersen/DSM-5 MCI so OVERESTIMATES MCI. Age gradient: 11% (65-74) rising to 30.3% (85+)
mci.citation: Graham JE, Rockwood K, Beattie BL, et al. Prevalence and severity of cognitive impairment with and without dementia in an elderly population. Lancet 1997;349(9068):1793-1796. Canadian Study of Health and Aging (CSHA). CIND 16.8% in 65+. Seed CSHA methods paper = CMAJ 1994 (PMID 7763211). No newer national MCI cohort estimate published for Canada; Canadian Task Force on Preventive Health Care and PLOS One 2020 (PMC7743951) both triangulate to this ~17% CSHA CIND figure and note published Canadian MCI cohort rates are unavailable.
mci.subnational: CSHA sampled 5 study regions (Atlantic, Quebec, Ontario, Prairies, BC) / 36 communities; no standardized province-level MCI prevalence
mci.representative: national (CSHA; community-dwelling + institutional 65+)
mci.n: CSHA ~10,263 screened (65+); CIND estimated within full-exam subsample

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (CLSA: 'Do you feel like your memory is becoming worse?'); single-item measure INFLATES prevalence (40-60% band); not SCD-I structured framework
scd.citation: Canadian Longitudinal Study on Aging (CLSA), single-item SCD measure. Cohort abstracts do not report a clean population SCD prevalence; ~54.7% derived (6890/12,603) from cognitively-healthy analytic subsample in Marinou et al., J Neuropsychol 2025 (Controls n=5713; SCD-no-worry n=3379; SCD+worry n=3511), consistent with single-item inflation. Seed: Hopper, Hammond, Stinchcombe 2021 (PMC8681321), analytic n=35,680. For triangulation, COSMIC harmonized international pooled SCD ~25.6% (95%CI 25.1-26.1), 16 cohorts/15 countries (PMC7749505). Need full text for CLSA-specific point prevalence.
scd.representative: national (CLSA community-dwelling 45-85); value derived from analytic subsample, not a published population estimate
scd.n: CLSA subsample ~12,603 (Marinou); seed cohort analytic n=35,680

mci.criteria: Petersen (EPIDEMCA; CSI-D + GMS-AGECAT + CERAD 10-word list)
mci.citation: EPIDEMCA programme (Pilleron S, et al.), population-based multicentre study in Central African Republic + Republic of Congo, urban+rural, aged >=65 (~2002 total). MCI (Petersen criteria) prevalence 7.2% in the Central African Republic (Bangui urban + Nola rural). Value surfaced consistently via EPIDEMCA outputs incl. British J Nutrition 2015 undernutrition paper (DOI 10.1017/S0007114515001749); confirm exact per-country split in full text. PMID 26099336.
mci.representative: community/near-national (Bangui urban + Nola rural, aged 65+)

mci.criteria: Petersen (EPIDEMCA; CSI-D + GMS-AGECAT + CERAD 10-word list)
mci.citation: EPIDEMCA programme (Pilleron S, et al.), population-based multicentre study in Central African Republic + Republic of Congo, urban+rural, aged >=65 (~2002 total). MCI (Petersen criteria) prevalence 6.1% in the Republic of Congo (Brazzaville urban + Gamboma rural). Value surfaced consistently via EPIDEMCA outputs incl. British J Nutrition 2015 undernutrition paper (DOI 10.1017/S0007114515001749); confirm exact per-country split in full text. PMID 26099336.
mci.representative: community/near-national (Brazzaville urban + Gamboma rural, aged 65+)

mci.criteria: SHARE cognitive-test battery harmonized across countries and validated against HCAP (not clinical MoCA/MMSE); Switzerland sits at the low end of the MCI distribution. NOTE: exact Switzerland table value not quoted in abstract - captured as ~17% low-end
mci.citation: Stephan BCM, Riedel-Heller SG, et al. Harmonized prevalence estimates of dementia in Europe vary strongly with childhood education. Scientific Reports 2025;15:14100. SHARE 2022 wave, n=47,773 aged 65+ across 27 European countries + Israel; validated vs HCAP. MCI range 17.2% (Sweden) - 31.1% (Portugal), mean ~24%; Switzerland has lowest dementia in Europe (4.5%) and is at the low end of MCI (~17%). Switzerland-specific MCI point value needs full-text/table confirmation. PubMed 40269049; PMC12019132. Supersedes Lc65+ 2022 seed (cohort profile, no MCI prevalence reported). Alt: Meier C, Wieczorek M, et al. Eur J Public Health 2025;35(1):134-140 (SHARE W8, n=1645, aged 58+, MCI=1.5 SD below age/education norm) confirms national MCI sample but reports associations only, no overall prevalence.
mci.representative: national (SHARE Switzerland nationally representative, aged 65+); pooled 27-country harmonized model - Switzerland subsample n not separately reported
mci.n: 47773 (pooled 27 countries; Switzerland subsample not separately stated)

scd.criteria: self-report via structured face-to-face interview (NOT SCD-I framework). Reported value is subjective memory decline (SMD, memory-specific self-report) = 40.0%; broad 'at least one cognitive difficulty' definition inflates to 93.6% - single-region self-report over-estimate
scd.citation: Fiordelli M, Albanese E, et al. The role of disability and depressive symptoms in the relation between objective cognitive performance and subjective cognitive decline (SwissDEM). Frontiers in Psychiatry 2022;13:963703. Randomized community sample n=250 aged >=65, Canton Ticino (Italian-speaking southern Switzerland), May 2021-Apr 2022, face-to-face interviews. 93.6% reported >=1 cognitive difficulty (broad SCD, inflated); 40.0% subjective memory decline (SMD). PMC9729556. Regional only (one canton), not nationally representative.
scd.representative: regional/community (Canton Ticino only; not nationally representative of Switzerland)

mci.criteria: MMSE + Pfeffer FAQ, suspected dementia excluded (not Petersen; MMSE-based combo)
mci.citation: Concha-Cisternas Y et al. Association of mild cognitive impairment and physical activity level in older persons in Chile. Aging and Health Research 2022. Chilean National Health Survey (ENS) 2016-17 subsample, n=1,981 aged 60+ (mean 70.7y, 63% women). MCI prevalence 10% (increasing with age), evaluated with MMSE + Pfeffer Functional Activities Questionnaire, excluding suspected dementia. Verified seed source.
mci.representative: national (ENS 2016-17 nationally representative subsample of adults 60+, n=1,981)

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (SMC: frequent memory failure / forgetting recent events or appointments); not SCD-I structured framework
scd.citation: Oyarzun-Gonzalez X, Abner EL, Toro P, Ferreccio C. Prevalence and Factors Associated with Subjective Memory Complaint in a Semi-Rural Community in Chile. J Alzheimers Dis 2023 (MAUCO/Maule Cohort baseline). n=6,687 aged 38-74. SMC overall 16.4%; 15.9% in 38-59; 17.6% (95%CI 15.8-19.4) in 60-74. Single/two-item self-report of memory complaint. Regional (Maule), not national.
scd.subnational: regional: 1 admin region (Maule, semi-rural Molina area)
scd.representative: regional (population-based community cohort, semi-rural Maule region; not nationally representative)

mci.criteria: MMSE cutoff (cognitive impairment; education/normative bias in low-schooling rural sample, not Petersen MCI)
mci.citation: Tianyi FL, et al. Factors Associated with the Prevalence of Cognitive Impairment in a Rural Elderly Cameroonian Population: A Community-Based Study in Sub-Saharan Africa. Dement Geriatr Cogn Disord. 2019. MMSE-defined cognitive impairment prevalence 33.3%; associated with older age, female sex, being single, lack of formal education, higher systolic BP. High figure reflects MMSE screening in a low-education rural population, not clinically-adjudicated Petersen MCI.
mci.representative: community (rural Cameroon, elderly)

mci.criteria: Petersen criteria (2-stage design: MMSE/CDR + clinical + neuropsychological battery); MCI diagnosed clinically, not MoCA-only
mci.citation: Jia L, Du Y, Chu L, et al. Prevalence, risk factors, and management of dementia and mild cognitive impairment in adults aged 60 years or older in China: a cross-sectional study (COAST / China Cognition and Aging Study). Lancet Public Health. 2020;5(12):e661-e671. Nationally representative, 96 sites across 12 provinces. MCI prevalence 15.5% (overall cognitive impairment 21.5% = 15.5% MCI + 6.0% dementia); ~38.77 million MCI cases estimated nationally. Exact 95% CI for the MCI point estimate not captured from abstract — need full text for interval.
mci.subnational: province/region level — 12 provinces sampled; paper reports breakdowns by economic region and urban vs rural residence
mci.representative: national — community-dwelling adults 60+, stratified multistage sampling across 12 provinces/96 sites

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item — pooled across 17 heterogeneous studies using predominantly self-report SCD instruments (not the structured SCD-I framework); estimate sits in the 40-60% self-report inflation band
scd.citation: High prevalence of subjective cognitive decline in older Chinese adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Front Public Health. 2023;11:1277995 (PMC10722401). 17 studies, 31,782 participants. Pooled SCD prevalence 46.4% (95% CI 40.6-52.2). Age subgroups: 60-69y 38.0%, 70-79y 45.2%, ≥80y 60.3%. Women 58.9% vs men 50.8%. High heterogeneity; self-report definitions inflate estimate.
scd.subnational: region level — North China 41.3% vs South China 50.0%; urban 47.1% vs rural 50.0% (subgroup pooled estimates, not admin-unit-level)
scd.representative: national — pooled meta-analysis of 17 community-based Chinese studies of older adults

mci.criteria: Abbreviated MMSE (AMMSE) score <13 = cognitive impairment (MMSE-based screening cutoff, NOT Petersen clinical MCI; conflates MCI+dementia so overstates pure MCI). Triangulation: same SABE 2015 survey yields screen-detected MCI 10.7% via shorter MMSE <=12/19 (PMC9623159, 2022), which is the cleaner MCI-specific estimate
mci.citation: Social patterning of cognitive impairment in Colombia: evidence from the SABE 2015 study. BMC Geriatrics 2024;24. SABE Colombia (National Survey of Health, Wellbeing & Aging), n=23,694 community-dwelling adults 60+. Prevalence ~19.7-20% cognitive impairment by AMMSE<13; strong social gradient (low education OR 3.01, illiteracy OR 2.14, dark skin OR 1.27). Alt MCI-specific: screen-detected MCI 10.7% (MMSE<=12/19) and 8.1% (SPMSQ) from same SABE data (PMC9623159, 2022)
mci.subnational: urban/rural strata (urban 72.5% / rural 27.5%); separate regional MCI studies exist (Southern Colombia, ADR-230041/PMC10789289; Bogota sample PMC5674670)
mci.representative: national — SABE Colombia is nationally representative stratified sample of community-dwelling adults 60+

scd.criteria: self-report subjective memory complaints (SMC); Colombia country subset of a 10-country Latin American/Caribbean cross-sectional study. Value 20.7% is moderate (not the 40-60% inflation typical of single-item SCD), but measure is self-report and sampling is not a national probability frame -> not SCD-I structured framework
scd.citation: Caldichoury N, et al. Subjective memory complaints in Latin American older adults: Prevalence and risk factors. J Alzheimers Dis 2025. Multi-country (10 LatAm/Caribbean) cross-sectional; Colombia subset SMC prevalence 20.7%. Colombia-specific n and CI not captured in abstract — need full text. No SABE-based national SCD prevalence exists; MCRS (slow gait + memory complaint) was 5.45% in SABE (PMC8680813) but requires gait, not pure SCD
scd.representative: regional — multi-country LatAm study; Colombia subset is community/convenience, COVID-era, NOT nationally representative

mci.criteria: 10/66 aMCI (Mayo Clinic amnestic MCI algorithm) — STRICT amnestic-only, UNDERESTIMATES all-type MCI. Pooled crude aMCI across 8 sites 0.8–4.3% (age/sex/education-standardized 0.6–4.6%); exact Cuba value in full-text Table 1 — need full text.
mci.citation: Sosa AL et al. Prevalence, Distribution, and Impact of Mild Cognitive Impairment in Latin America, China, and India: A 10/66 Population-Based Study. PLOS Med. 2012;9(2):e1001170. Cuba = nationally-representative 10/66 catchment. Need full text for exact Cuba aMCI %.
mci.representative: national (10/66 Cuba, community 65+)

mci.criteria: Harmonized SHARE cognitive screen (SHARE-Cog 3-item, validated vs HCAP) — screening-based, likely OVERESTIMATES clinical MCI; value approximate ('almost a third')
mci.citation: SHARE Wave 2022 harmonized estimates (Bacher/Börsch-Supan et al., Scientific Reports 2025). MCI for 27 EU countries + Israel; Cyprus named among the highest, 'almost a third' (~30%). Exact per-country Cyprus value not in abstract — need full-text/supplement.
mci.representative: national (SHARE, age 65+; part of 47,773 pooled)

mci.criteria: composite SHARE psychometric cutoff (verbal fluency + immediate recall + delayed recall + temporal orientation); NOT Petersen/MoCA/MMSE — labelled 'cognitive impairment', a proxy for MCI rather than clinical MCI
mci.citation: Seblova D, Brayne C, Machu V, Kuklova M, Kopecek M, Cermakova P. Changes in Cognitive Impairment in the Czech Republic. J Alzheimers Dis. 2019;72(3):693-701. National SHARE-CZ cohort: age-specific prevalence of cognitive impairment declined from 11% in 2006/07 (wave 2, n=1,107) to 9% in 2015 (wave 6, n=3,104), most conservative estimate. Seed verified. Triangulation: harmonized SHARE-2022 estimates (Rohr et al., Sci Rep 2025, PMC12019132) give European MCI 17.2-31.1% with Czechia representing Eastern Europe but no Czech-specific value extractable; broader MCI operationalization explains the higher range.
mci.access: OA (PMC6845821 conference abstract carries the number); full JAD article paywalled(IOS Press)
mci.representative: national — Czech Republic SHARE sample, community-dwelling adults 50+

mci.criteria: MCI original concept (Petersen-based, operationalized via SIDAM neuropsychological testing); modified concept 25.2% (23.7-26.7). NOT MoCA (no MoCA overestimation).
mci.citation: Luck T, Riedel-Heller SG, et al. Mild Cognitive Impairment in General Practice: Age-Specific Prevalence and Correlate Results from the German Study on Ageing, Cognition and Dementia in Primary Care Patients (AgeCoDe). Dement Geriatr Cogn Disord 2007;24(4):307-316. GP-registered patients aged 75+, n=3327. Prevalence 15.4% (original MCI concept) / 25.2% (modified concept). This is the actual prevalence source, improving on the seed 2014 Annals of Family Medicine paper (Kaduszkiewicz, annfammed 12/2/158) which reports MCI prognosis (n=357 MCI, ~1/4 progress to dementia in 3y), not population prevalence.
mci.representative: clinic/primary-care; GP-registered patients aged 75+ recruited across 6 German cities (Bonn, Duesseldorf, Hamburg, Leipzig, Mannheim, Munich) - large multicenter but not nationally representative general population; PubMed 17848793

scd.criteria: self-report memory-decline question within SCD-I/AgeCoDe framework; falls in the inflated 40-60% self-report band (broad definition, any perceived decline)
scd.citation: AgeCoDe (German Study on Ageing, Cognition and Dementia in Primary Care Patients): 702/1387 (50.6%) of cognitively-unimpaired older adults (mean age 82.2) reported subjective cognitive decline at baseline; incident SCD 24.0% (233/971) at follow-up (Roehr S, Jessen F, et al., PLOS One 2016). SCD single/broad self-report inflates to 40-60%. Triangulation: COSMIC international pooled SCD ~23.8% (qualitative harmonization) to 25.6% (IRT) across cohorts (Roehr 2020, Alz Res Ther, PMC7749505); German population-based domain-specific rates memory 65.9% down to language 31.5% (Roehr 2020, Int J Geriatr Psychiatry gps.5359). Need full text for exact CI on the 50.6% baseline figure.
scd.representative: clinic/primary-care; cognitively-unimpaired GP patients aged 75+ across 6 German cities (multicenter, not nationally representative)

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item
scd.citation: Waldorff FB, Siersma V, Waldemar G. Subjective memory complaints in primary care patients and death from all causes: A four-year follow-up. Scand J Prim Health Care. 2013;31(1). Of 758 community-dwelling primary-care patients aged 65+ in central Copenhagen, 177 (24%; 177/758 = 23.4%) reported subjective memory complaints via a single yes/no question. Single-item self-report inflates SCD; single-district primary-care sample, not nationally representative. DOI 10.3109/02813432.2012.754092.
scd.representative: clinic; single-district Copenhagen primary-care sample of community-dwellers, not nationally representative

mci.criteria: 10/66 aMCI (Mayo Clinic amnestic MCI algorithm) — strict amnestic-only, UNDERESTIMATES all-type MCI. Pooled crude aMCI 0.8–4.3% (standardized 0.6–4.6%) across sites; exact Dominican Republic value in full-text Table 1 — need full text.
mci.citation: Sosa AL et al. Prevalence, Distribution, and Impact of Mild Cognitive Impairment in Latin America, China, and India: A 10/66 Population-Based Study. PLOS Med. 2012;9(2):e1001170. DR = 10/66 Santo Domingo urban catchment. Need full text for exact DR aMCI %.
mci.representative: regional (10/66 Dominican Republic — Santo Domingo urban, community 65+)

mci.criteria: clinical diagnosis (Petersen-type) via door-to-door survey — MCI = 15 cases (11 amnestic multi-domain, 4 amnestic single-domain); prev derived as 15/~305; note dementia 5.28%
mci.citation: Sidi M'Hamed Algerian Study, door-to-door survey in Sidi M'Hamed district (Algiers), 2012-2014; non-demented cognitive impairment 37.05% (113 cases incl. normal aging), of which 15 MCI cases => ~4.9% MCI. Derived value; need full text for exact denominator/DOI
mci.representative: regional/community (Sidi M'Hamed district, Algiers)

mci.criteria: MMSE + AD8 informant questionnaire. Reported figure is COMBINED 'cognitive impairment + possible dementia' 18–21% at age 65 (rising to 54–60% at 85) — NOT isolated MCI, so OVERESTIMATES MCI. Seed Atahualpa Project used MoCA (also overestimates MCI ~20%+). No clean isolated-MCI point estimate exists for Ecuador.
mci.citation: Orozco-Hernández JP et al. Cognitive Decline in Adults Aged 65+ in Cumbayá, Quito, Ecuador: Prevalence and Risk Factors. Cureus 2018 (MMSE+AD8, n=144, mean age 75.3). Also Atahualpa Project (rural coastal Ecuador, MoCA-based cognitive dysfunction), scielo.senescyt.gob.ec S2631-25812017000100164.
mci.representative: community (Cumbayá, Quito) + rural (Atahualpa) — regional, not national; small n

mci.criteria: DSM-IV clinical dx after 2-stage door-to-door screen (Memory & Executive Screening [MES] + MMSE); MMSE-based screening underestimates MCI -> conservative value
mci.citation: Khedr EM, Fawi G, Abbas MAA, Mohammed TA, Abo El-Fetoh N, Al Attar G, Noaman M, Zaki AF. Prevalence of Mild Cognitive Impairment and Dementia among the Elderly Population of Qena Governorate, Upper Egypt: A Community-Based Study. J Alzheimers Dis. 2015;45(1):117-126. Door-to-door survey, n=691 aged >=60; 12 MCI cases -> crude prevalence 1.74/100. TRIANGULATION: newer MoCA-based community study (Kafr El-Sheikh, J Egypt Public Health Assoc 2024, n=470, DOI 10.1186/s42506-023-00147-3) reports 37.7% MCI (MoCA overestimates); true Egypt value likely between ~2% and ~38%. No national ageing-cohort MCI figure (AL-SEHA is pilot only).
mci.access: paywalled(SAGE/IOS Press); free full text at sohag-univ.edu.eg
mci.subnational: governorate level (Qena, 1 of 27 governorates, Upper Egypt)
mci.representative: regional (single governorate, community-dwelling >=60, door-to-door)

mci.criteria: Petersen (subjective cognitive concern + objective impairment in >=1 domain + preserved function + no dementia); NOT MoCA-only. Overall value is for >=50y; substantially higher in 65+ and in women
mci.citation: Lara E, Koyanagi A, Olaya B, Lobo A, Miret M, Tyrovolas S, Ayuso-Mateos JL, Haro JM. Mild cognitive impairment in a Spanish representative sample: prevalence and associated factors. Int J Geriatr Psychiatry. 2016;31(8):858-867. Overall MCI prevalence 9.6% among >=50y (N=3625), nationally representative COURAGE in Europe / Edad con Salud sample; higher in women and older ages. Regional triangulation: DERIVA (Salamanca, 65+) ~18.5%. DOI likely 10.1002/gps.4399 - need full text to confirm.
mci.subnational: Regional single-province studies exist: DERIVA (Salamanca, 65+, MCI/cognitive impairment ~11.6% at 65-69 rising to ~22.9% at 85+); ZARADEMP (Zaragoza); Gomez de Caso sentinel-network study. No official national admin-level MCI registry.
mci.representative: national - nationally representative Spanish community-dwelling sample >=50y (COURAGE in Europe / Edad con Salud project)

scd.ci: sex-specific reported: women 31.2%, men 22.1% (combined ~27% derived; paper reports by sex, not a single pooled overall)
scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (EHSS20 / EHIS memory-and-concentration difficulty question) - INFLATES; NOT SCD-I structured framework
scd.citation: Subjective Cognitive Impairment and Physical Activity: Investigating Risk Factors and Correlations among Older Adults in Spain. J Funct Morphol Kinesiol (MDPI). 2024;9(3):150 (PMC11417891). EHSS20 European Health Survey in Spain, N=7082 aged >=65: women 31.2% vs men 22.1% subjective cognitive impairment (~27% combined). Single self-report item, likely inflated. More reliable structured triangulation: ALFA cohort (Catalonia, middle-aged, population-based) SCD 21.4% (572/2670); COSMIC international pooled SCD-I 23.8% (qualitative) - 25.6% (IRT).
scd.subnational: Regional: ALFA cohort (Catalonia/Barcelona, BarcelonaBeta), middle-aged cognitively unimpaired, structured SCD prevalence 21.4%.
scd.representative: national - nationally representative Spanish survey (EHSS20) aged >=65; but measure is a single self-report item, so prevalence should be treated as an upper-inflated estimate

mci.age: 50+ (mature and older adults, defined as >=50 yrs)
mci.criteria: SMMSE with education-adjusted cutoff (<=grade8: <=22/30; >=grade9: <=24/30). NOTE: measures TOTAL cognitive impairment, NOT Petersen/DSM-5 MCI - conflates MCI with dementia and overestimates in a low-education population; treat as upper bound, not clean MCI.
mci.citation: Gela YY, Fekadu SA, Belsti Y, et al. Cognitive impairment and associated factors among mature and older adults living in the community of Gondar town, Ethiopia, 2020. Sci Rep. 2022;12:7545. SMMSE cognitive impairment 43.8% (95% CI 38.8-48.7); this is total cognitive impairment (MCI+dementia), NOT Petersen MCI. Triangulation: Africa MCI systematic review/meta-analysis pooled prevalence 29.39% (Asnakew S, et al. Magnitude and predictors of mild cognitive impairment among older populations in Africa. Transl Psychiatry. 2025; doi:10.1038/s41398-025-03620-z) - regional, not Ethiopia-specific.
mci.representative: community - single urban town (Gondar, Northwest Ethiopia), single-stage cluster sample; not nationally representative

mci.criteria: Mayo Clinic/Petersen MCI criteria applied via neuropsychological test battery + modified Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR); NOT MoCA-only, so not inflated
mci.citation: Hänninen T, Hallikainen M, Tuomainen S, Vanhanen M, Soininen H. Prevalence of mild cognitive impairment: a population-based study in elderly subjects. Acta Neurol Scand. 2002;106(3):148-154. Population-based random sample, city of Kuopio, eastern Finland; n=806 evaluated (of 1150 sampled), aged 60-76; MCI prevalence 5.3% by Mayo/Petersen criteria. Full DOI (10.1034/j.1600-0404.2002.01225.x) not independently verified — PubMed abstract used.
mci.representative: regional/community — single city (Kuopio), eastern Finland; population-based random sample, not nationally representative

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (subjective memory complaint, present in 76.3%). NOTE: this is from an AAMI study — the AAMI syndrome (NIMH criteria, which ADDS an objective memory-decline criterion and is therefore NOT pure SCD) was 53.8% inclusion-only and 38.4% with exclusions. Single-item self-report figure is heavily inflated.
scd.citation: Koivisto K, Reinikainen KJ, Hänninen T, et al. Prevalence of age-associated memory impairment in a randomly selected population from eastern Finland. Neurology. 1995;45(4):741-747. n=1049 aged 60-78; subjective memory impairment (single self-report) 76.3%; AAMI (NIMH criteria) 53.8% inclusion-only, 38.4% with exclusions. No structured SCD-I framework used.
scd.access: paywalled(Wolters Kluwer/Lippincott) — abstract free on PubMed
scd.representative: regional/community — eastern Finland, randomly selected population sample, not nationally representative

mci.criteria: amnestic MCI — memory complaint + objective memory impairment, no dementia/no ADL disability (neuropsychological test battery, NOT MoCA/MMSE-only); Ritchie/pre-Petersen-2004 operationalization. Narrow criteria markedly underestimate broad all-cause MCI (intl 65+ pooled ~15-20%).
mci.citation: Larrieu S, Letenneur L, Orgogozo JM, Fabrigoule C, Amieva H, Le Carret N, Barberger-Gateau P, Dartigues JF. Incidence and outcome of mild cognitive impairment in a population-based prospective cohort. Neurology 2002;59(10):1594-1599. Baseline MCI prevalence 2.8% (58 prevalent cases); 5-yr incidence 9.9/1000 person-years (1265 at risk); highly unstable (>40% reverted to normal). PAQUID cohort, community-dwelling elderly, Gironde & Dordogne departments, SW France. Abstract carries the numbers; full text paywalled.
mci.access: paywalled(Wolters Kluwer / AAN Neurology); abstract has values
mci.representative: regional (Gironde & Dordogne, SW France); population-based community-dwelling >=65 (PAQUID). Not a national sample.
mci.n: ~2071 prevalence-screen sample (58/0.028); 1265 at risk for 5-yr incidence

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (subjective memory impairment / single memory-complaint question). Single-item self-report can inflate SCD; here value is moderate and triangulates with COSMIC pooled SCD 23.8-25.6% (COSMIC includes 3C). Not the structured SCD-I framework.
scd.citation: Genziani M, Stewart R, Bejot Y, Amieva H, Artero S, Ritchie K. Subjective memory impairment, objective cognitive functioning and social activity in French older people: findings from the Three Cities (3C) study. Geriatr Gerontol Int 2013;13(1):139-145. Subjective memory impairment prevalence 21.9% in adults >=65; population-based 3C cohort (Bordeaux, Dijon, Montpellier, n~9294). Free INSERM HAL postprint: https://inserm.hal.science/inserm-00914850/document . PubMed 22672286.
scd.access: OA (INSERM HAL green postprint free full text); journal paywalled(Wiley)
scd.representative: community/regional — 3 French cities (Bordeaux, Dijon, Montpellier); population-based random sample of >=65, urban older French. Large but not strictly national.

mci.criteria: CFAS consensus 7-category framework; reported numeric = 'cognitive impairment' (MMSE<24, no functional impairment) category ~= CIND, MMSE-based (MMSE UNDERESTIMATES MCI); pure consensus-criteria MCI reported only as 'stable' 1991->2011, exact % needs full text. TRAP: MRC-CFAS mapping of 18 MCI definitions gave prevalence 0.1%-42.0% (Stephan 2007) -> extremely criteria-sensitive
mci.citation: Cognitive Function and Ageing Studies (CFAS I/II). Two-decade change in prevalence of cognitive impairment in the UK. Eur J Epidemiol 2019;34:1085-1092. England 65+, CFAS II n=7796 (2011). NOTE: abstract gives 'cognitive impairment'(CIND, MMSE-based) 6.3% (654,436 cases) 2011 vs 13.5% 1991; clean pure-MCI point % needs full text.
mci.subnational: 3 CFAS sentinel areas (Cambridgeshire, Newcastle, Nottingham); reported pooled/weighted, not standard admin-unit breakdown
mci.representative: national (England 65+); CFAS II weighted-representative sample across 3 areas; n=7796

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (simple SCD [sSCD] = subjective report of worsening memory or concentration); NOT SCD-I structured framework. TRAP: single-item self-report inflates SCD; men-only cohort -> confidence low
scd.citation: Predictors and prognosis of population-based subjective cognitive decline: longitudinal evidence from the Caerphilly Prospective Study (CaPS). BMJ Open 2023 (PMC10582873). n=1225 men, Caerphilly/South Wales; sSCD prevalence ~30%. Triangulates vs COSMIC international SCD pooled 23.8-25.6% (Rohr 2020, Alzheimers Res Ther, doi:10.1186/s13195-020-00734-y).
scd.subnational: Wales (Caerphilly, South Wales) - single locality, men only
scd.representative: regional/community (one South Wales town, men only) - NOT nationally representative; single-item + male-only bias

mci.age: community-dwelling adults (age band not specified in abstract; MCI associated with age >65, urban residence, low education, hypertension); amnestic MCI 9.3%
mci.criteria: Georgian-version MoCA + cognitive-domain index score; population-based (more rigorous than MoCA-only cutoff)
mci.citation: Janelidze M, et al. 2018, 'Mild Cognitive Impairment in Republic of Georgia', Gerontology & Geriatric Medicine — first population-based MCI study in Georgia; 1000 screened, 851 met inclusion
mci.subnational: urban vs rural strata reported (urban residence a risk factor)
mci.representative: national (first population-based MCI prevalence for Georgia)

mci.criteria: MoCA<26 only (no education/normative adjustment; massively overestimates MCI)
mci.citation: Exploration of demographic prevalence of mild cognitive impairment using Montreal Cognitive Assessment: a cross-sectional pilot study in the Cape Coast Metropolis, Ghana. 2024. n=100, MoCA<26; 42.86% of males and 40.54% of females classified MCI (~41-43% overall); 65.4% in the 40-49y subgroup. WEAK SOURCE: small convenience/pilot sample, includes non-elderly adults, MoCA-only cutoff strongly education-confounded (r=0.608 with MoCA score) -> large overestimate. Not population-representative; prefer regional pooled for Ghana modelling.
mci.representative: convenience/clinic pilot (Cape Coast, n=100, ages 40+, not representative)

mci.criteria: Petersen criteria (MCI) — full neurological + neuropsychological assessment by multidisciplinary team (NOT MoCA-only, so no ~20% overestimation); amnestic & multidomain subtypes most common
mci.citation: Vlachos GS, Kosmidis MH, Yannakoulia M, et al. Prevalence of Mild Cognitive Impairment in the Elderly Population in Greece: Results From the HELIAD Study. Alzheimer Dis Assoc Disord. 2020 (PubMed 31913961). Age- and sex-standardized MCI prevalence = 13.11% in >=65y; n=1960 randomly selected. 95% CI not given in abstract (need full text). HELIAD = Hellenic Longitudinal Investigation of Aging and Diet, population-based cohort.
mci.access: paywalled(Wolters Kluwer/Ovid); abstract carries the 13.11% value
mci.subnational: regional geographic distribution + 1991-2050 projections available in secondary analysis (Brain Sci. 2025;15(6):661, MDPI/PMC12191139, MCI & dementia across Greek regions); HELIAD itself sampled 2 catchment areas (Marousi/Attica urban + Larissa/Thessaly rural)
mci.representative: national — population-based, representative of Greek >=65 population (HELIAD, random selection)

scd.criteria: self-report; single general memory-decline question = 28.0% (reported as primary comparable value). Structured multi-item self-report questionnaire (NOT the SCD-I diagnostic framework) inflates: >=1 subjective memory complaint = 76.6%; >=1 deficit in ANY cognitive domain = 84.2%; naming 26.0%, orientation 5.4%, calculation 2.6%. Assessed only in cognitively-normal (non-MCI/non-dementia) subsample.
scd.citation: Vlachos GS, Cosentino S, Kosmidis MH, et al. Prevalence and determinants of subjective cognitive decline in a representative Greek elderly population. Int J Geriatr Psychiatry. 2019;34(6):846-854 (PubMed 30714214). n=1456 cognitively-normal >=65y (HELIAD cohort). Single general memory question 28.0%; >=1 memory complaint 76.6%; >=1 cognitive-domain deficit 84.2%. This SUPERSEDES the audit's SCD seed (HELIAD SCD-frailty, PubMed 30621435) which examines SCD->frailty association and does NOT report a clean SCD point-prevalence. DOI not verified — using PubMed stable URL.
scd.access: paywalled(Wiley/Int J Geriatr Psychiatry); abstract carries the percentages
scd.representative: national — population-based, representative of Greek >=65 (HELIAD cognitively-normal subsample)

mci.criteria: Clinical screening for deterioro cognitivo leve (MCI) among hospital outpatients (16/119 = 13.4%; likely MMSE/MoCA-based clinical assessment). CLINIC sample, not population — selection bias likely; small n.
mci.citation: Determinación de deterioro cognitivo en adulto mayor, consulta externa Hospital Regional de Zacapa. Revista Científica Sociedad CUNZAC. 2022. (n=119 outpatients aged 60–89; 16 MCI, 5 dementia-range).
mci.representative: clinic (Zacapa outpatient department) — NOT representative

mci.criteria: Harmonized composite (Langa-Weir Lscore 13.2% / HCAP-validated Hscore 9.0% / general Gscore 5.4%); MCI=cognitively-impaired-not-demented. IMPORTANT: value is cross-European POOLED, not Hungary-specific; scoring-dependent. No MoCA/MMSE community figure for Hungary.
mci.citation: Borsch-Supan et al., 'Harmonized prevalence estimates of dementia in Europe vary strongly with childhood education', Sci Rep 2025 (SHARE Wave 9 HCAP-validated; 47,773 adults 65+, 27 European countries + Israel incl. Hungary). Cross-European pooled cognitive-impairment prevalence 5.4-13.2% depending on score; Hungary IS one of the 27 modeled countries but NO Hungary-specific MCI point value is in the abstract (need full text). Only actual Hungary-specific MCI number: residential-care cohort Kalman et al. 2007 (n~2100) ~18% MCI — institutional, non-representative, OVERESTIMATES community MCI (see https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17562518/). Context: Hungarian dementia registry (Sci Rep 2021, s41598-021-89179-3) standardized dementia 60+ = 7.1-7.3%.
mci.representative: national — SHARE Wave 9 covers Hungary among 27 countries, but reported value is cross-European pooled; Hungary-specific MCI value needs full text

mci.criteria: Self-reported difficulty with memory or concentration (Susenas single-item; multinomial normal/MCI/dementia classification). NOT Petersen/DSM-5 neuropsychological MCI and no MoCA/MMSE — self-report functional proxy; likely conservative vs clinical MCI (disability-survey framing understates rather than the MoCA-overestimate trap).
mci.citation: Rismawaty et al. Determinants of Cognitive Impairment among The Elderly in Indonesia. Jurnal Berkala Kesehatan (2021). Underlying data = Susenas September 2018 (BPS / Statistics Indonesia), n=26,010 community-dwelling adults aged >=60, national. Reported MCI 17.08%, dementia 0.86%. CORRECTION: prior audit labeled this 'IFLS-based (2021)' but the data source is Susenas (govt national survey), not IFLS. Best available Indonesia-wide 'MCI' figure but is a self-report proxy, not a neuropsychological diagnosis.
mci.representative: national (Susenas 2018 nationally representative socioeconomic survey; community-dwelling >=60)

mci.criteria: Petersen-style modified: MoCA <26 + subjective cognitive complaints + MMSE >=14 + no ADL limitation. Tool = MoCA (cutoff <26, LIBERAL -> overestimation risk) plus MMSE floor; constrained by complaint + intact-ADL requirements which pulls estimate down to a plausible 10.1%.
mci.citation: Mild cognitive impairment is associated with falls among older adults: Findings from The Irish Longitudinal Study on Ageing (TILDA). Maturitas 2016. MCI prevalence 10.1% in n=5364 community-dwelling adults aged >=50, TILDA Wave 1. PubMed 26707711.
mci.representative: national

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (self-rated memory 'fair or poor'). NOT SCD-I structured framework. Single-item framing is conservative ('fair/poor' threshold) so figure is NOT inflated to the typical 40-60% seen with 'any memory complaint' items.
scd.citation: TILDA Wave 4 Key Findings Report, Chapter 8 (Cognitive change over time), Trinity College Dublin. Self-reported fair/poor memory 15.1% at Wave 1 increasing to 19.5% at Wave 4; nationally representative cohort n=8175 aged >=50.
scd.representative: national

mci.criteria: Clinical/Petersen-type MCI classification via comprehensive home-visit assessment; specific screening instrument (MoCA/MMSE) NOT stated in abstract — tool unverified, so under/over-estimation cannot be excluded
mci.citation: Jerusalem Longitudinal Study — 'Dementia and Mild Cognitive Impairment: Prevalence, Conversion Rates, and Longevity Between Ages 70 and 95', Innovation in Aging 2023;7(Suppl 1):743 (GSA annual meeting abstract). Prospective representative community-dwelling cohort born 1920-21, Jerusalem; n per wave 437/806/1170/584/468 at ages 70/78/85/90/95. MCI prevalence 13.5%/21.5%/38.7%/37.0%/50%. NOTE: conference abstract (not full peer-reviewed article); best-available Israeli MCI trajectory. Triangulation: Afgin/Inzelberg JAD 2012 (Wadi Ara, N. Israel Arab villages) 32.1% MCI — low-education ethnic subgroup, overestimate, not nationally representative.
mci.representative: regional/community (community-dwelling Jerusalem single-birth-cohort b.1920-21; not national admin-level; no sub-national breakdown)

mci.criteria: DSM-5 mild NCD (neuropsych testing + informant reports, CDR-adjudicated algorithm; NOT MoCA-only, so not over-inflated)
mci.citation: LASI-DAD study. Prevalence of DSM-5 mild and major neurocognitive disorder in India: Results from the LASI-DAD. PLOS ONE 2024;19(2):e0297220. Mild NCD 17.6% (population-weighted) among adults 60+; major NCD 7.2%. Nationally representative multistage area-probability sample, N=4096. Exact 95% CI not shown in abstract preview - need full text.
mci.representative: national

scd.criteria: structured-questionnaire (SMCQ threshold); self-report — sits in inflated 40-60% band
scd.citation: Menon et al. Prevalence of subjective cognitive decline and its association with physical health problems among urban community-dwelling elderly in South India. Alzheimer's & Dementia 2025;21(2):e14505. SCD 45.2% (182/403), age 60+, single urban site, SMCQ tool. TRIANGULATION (lower/more balanced): Annals of Indian Academy of Neurology Jan 2025 rural-vs-urban study reports overall SCD 21.5% (urban 7.2%, rural 23.4%) and MCI 14.6% — indicates the single-site 45.2% is inflated.
scd.representative: community (single urban site, South India); not national

mci.criteria: cognitive-impairment screening (broad CI, NOT MCI-specific; ~65% of cases graded mild severity)
mci.citation: Prevalence of Cognitive Impairment Amongst Older Adults and the Associated Risk Factors (Sulaymaniyah, urban, Kurdistan Region), Kurdistan J Appl Res 2024; community cross-sectional 2022-2023, 400 adults >=60, cognitive impairment 31.25%
mci.representative: regional/community (Sulaymaniyah urban area, Kurdistan Region of Iraq)

mci.criteria: MMSE (literate) + AMTS (illiterate) screening; NOT Petersen/DSM-5 — screening-tool-based. MMSE tends to underestimate MCI; illiterate subgroup via AMTS ran very high (36.4%) vs literate 6.3%. Overall 15.8%.
mci.citation: Delbari A, et al. Prevalence and associated factors of mild cognitive impairment among middle-aged and older adults: Results of the first phase of Ardakan Cohort Study on Aging (ACSA). Health Sci Rep. 2024;7(1):e1827. Overall MCI 15.8% (literate 6.3%, illiterate 36.4%); n=4938 community-dwelling adults aged >=50; single city Ardakan (Yazd province), a subset of the Iranian Longitudinal Study on Aging (IRLSA). Closely matches WHO LMIC pooled ~15.3%.
mci.representative: community (single city: Ardakan, Yazd province; subset of national IRLSA framework, not nationally representative)

mci.criteria: Consensus MCI diagnosis per international/Petersen-type guidelines: cognitive impairment in memory or another domain (or >=2 domains) without loss of ADL/dementia. Three-step ascertainment — MMSE + Digit Symbol Substitution Test screening, then full neuropsychological battery, then expert panel (geriatrician/neurologist/neuropsychologist/neuroradiologist). NOT MoCA-based, so no MoCA overestimation; robust consensus method.
mci.citation: AGES-Reykjavik Study (Age Gene/Environment Susceptibility-Reykjavik), population-based cohort. Cross-sectional: 485/5169 (9.4%) MCI, 307/5169 (5.9%) dementia, mean age 76. Value reported in 'Associations of fat and muscle tissue with cognitive status in older adults: the AGES-Reykjavik Study' (2017), PMC5386007; identical cohort/diagnostic procedure summarized in seed overview 'Cognition and brain health among older adults in Iceland: the AGES-Reykjavik study', GeroScience 2022 (PMC9768066, DOI 10.1007/s11357-022-00642-z).
mci.representative: regional (Reykjavik capital-area population-based cohort; ~2/3 of Iceland's population lives in the capital region, so broadly generalizable to older Icelanders nationally)

mci.criteria: Petersen-type MCI operationalized on neuropsychological battery (global cognition, memory, attention) — NOT MoCA/MMSE-only; companion CIND estimate 9.5%. Figure sits at upper end of typical MCI range but uses full neuropsych battery, not a MoCA overestimate.
mci.citation: Di Carlo A, Lamassa M, Baldereschi M, Inzitari D, Scafato E, Farchi G, Inzitari D; Italian Longitudinal Study on Aging (ILSA) Working Group. CIND and MCI in the Italian elderly: frequency, vascular risk factors, progression to dementia. Neurology. 2007;69(23):2186-2185. n=2,830 aged 65-84 across 8 Italian municipalities; MCI prevalence 16.1% (CIND 9.5%), ~1,860,000 projected prevalent cases; MCI incidence 21.5/1,000 person-yr. NOTE: prior-audit seed URL n.neurology.org/content/68/22/1909 does not match this paper; verified article is Neurology 69(23):2186.
mci.representative: national — community-based multicenter (8 Italian municipalities, north/central/south), aged 65-84

scd.criteria: structured-questionnaire — AACD (Aging-Associated Cognitive Decline, Levy 1994) full criteria: requires subjective complaint of decline PLUS objective test performance >=1 SD below age norm. This is a STRUCTURED construct (better than single self-report) but NOT pure SCD-I: because it demands objective impairment it undercounts subjective-only decline. Broader AACD+AACD-3 (>=3 of 5 criteria) merged = 28.0%.
scd.citation: Solfrizzi V, et al.; Italian PRoject on Epidemiology of Alzheimer's disease (IPREA). Prevalence of aging-associated cognitive decline in an Italian elderly population: results from cross-sectional phase of IPREA. Aging Clin Exp Res. 2010;22(5-6):440-449. PubMed 20383053. n=4,785 aged 65-84 from registries of 12 Italian municipalities; full AACD 9.2% (274 subjects), AACD-3 18.8%, merged AACD+AACD-3 28.0% (M 28.3%, F 27.6%). CAVEAT: AACD is a subjective+objective construct, not pure SCD. Triangulation for pure subjective SCD: COSMIC pooled (Röhr 2020, Alzheimers Res Ther, incl. Italian InveCe.Ab cohort) ~23.8-25.6% across cohorts.
scd.representative: national — community-based multicenter (12 Italian municipalities), aged 65-84

mci.criteria: MMSE-based TOTAL cognitive impairment ('less than one in three' ≈ <30%), NOT isolated MCI — conflates MCI + dementia; MMSE underestimates MCI specifically but total-CI figure ~30% overstates MCI. Nationally representative sample. No isolated-MCI point estimate reported.
mci.citation: Mitchell-Fearon K et al. The Prevalence of Cognitive Impairment among Older Adults in Jamaica. WIMJ Open. 2015. National sample n=2,943 across 4 parishes, MMSE; <1 in 3 had some cognitive impairment.
mci.subnational: parish-level (4 parishes sampled)
mci.representative: national (community-dwelling 60+, 4 parishes) — but total CI, not MCI

mci.criteria: ECAQ (Elderly Cognitive Assessment Questionnaire; a dementia/CI screen, NOT MCI-specific — likely underestimates the MCI band)
mci.citation: Assessment of Cognitive Impairment and Related Factors Among Elderly People in Jordan (Jerash governorate), Iran J Nurs Midwifery Res 2024; household face-to-face, 220 adults >=60, ECAQ cognitive impairment 9.10%
mci.representative: regional/community (Jerash governorate, northern Jordan)

mci.criteria: Petersen; expert-adjudicated clinical diagnosis (MMSE + neuropsych battery + CDR, reviewed by psychiatrists/neurologists across 8 sites) — NOT MoCA-only, so no MoCA over-estimation
mci.citation: Ninomiya T, et al. Study design and baseline characteristics of a population-based prospective cohort study of dementia in Japan: the Japan Prospective Studies Collaboration for Aging and Dementia (JPSC-AD). Environ Health Prev Med. 2020;25:64. Crude baseline prevalence: MCI 17.0%, dementia 8.5%, in ~10,000 community-dwelling adults aged 65+ across 8 population-based sites; highest MCI prevalence in ages 80-84, MCI higher in men. Triangulation: 4-town ILSA-J (Hisayama/Nakajima/Nakayama/Ama, 2022-2023) pooled MCI 15.5% — consistent.
mci.subnational: 8 site/municipal cohorts pooled (e.g. Hisayama, Arao, Nakajima); not full 47-prefecture coverage
mci.representative: national (multisite population-based, 8 sites nationwide); community-dwelling 65+

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (subjective memory complaints / SMC, single question) — sits in the 40-60% inflation band; NOT SCD-I structured framework
scd.citation: Japan Gerontological Evaluation Study (JAGES) 2010, n=16,184 community-dwelling adults aged 65+ across 28 municipalities: subjective memory complaints (single-item self-report) prevalence 47.4% (reported in the childhood-SES/SMC analysis, Int Psychogeriatr, Cambridge). Prior-audit seed Nakamura et al. 2020 (Healthcare, PMC7551000; PMID 32962150) is a small non-representative convenience sample (n=285, Yasu City, Shiga, recruited via fliers) examining SCD correlates rather than a population prevalence, so JAGES is preferred for representativeness. No SCD-I structured-framework national estimate located; single-item SMC over-estimates true SCD.
scd.representative: near-national (28 municipalities, community-dwelling 65+; large sample) but single-item SMC inflates estimate

mci.criteria: Mixed Petersen/clinical criteria + neuropsychological cut-offs; pooled across 28 studies. NP-cutoff-only studies reported ~3x higher MCI than clinical-criteria studies; extreme heterogeneity (I2 ~99%). Tool varies (MMSE/MoCA/NP batteries).
mci.citation: Magnitude and predictors of mild cognitive impairment among older populations in Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Translational Psychiatry (2025). 28 studies, 9 African countries, 26,067 participants (MCI subset n=23,749; 4,762 cases). Africa-pooled MCI 20% (95% CI 14-28%). NOT Kenya-specific: no national/community MCI prevalence value identified for Kenya (best Kenya cognition data = DEM-SKY Makueni dementia screening, CSI-D, not MCI). Used as regional proxy.
mci.representative: regional — Africa-pooled meta-analysis (9 countries), NOT Kenya-specific; no Kenya national MCI survey exists
mci.n: 26,067 (9 countries, 28 studies); MCI subset 23,749 participants, 4,762 cases

scd.criteria: self-report / structured-questionnaire, heterogeneous across studies; not SCD-I structured framework. Single self-report items inflate SCD toward 40-60% — pooled 50% consistent with known inflation.
scd.year: 2026 (EA Health Research Journal; primary studies span multiple years)
scd.citation: Cognitive Ageing in Africa: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Pre-Dementia Prevalence and Associated Factors. EA Health Research Journal (2026). Same evidence base (28 studies, 9 African countries, 26,067 participants); SCD subset n=2,318, 1,162 cases. Africa-pooled SCD 50% (95% CI 40-59%). NOT Kenya-specific: no Kenya national/community SCD prevalence identified. Used as regional proxy.
scd.representative: regional — Africa-pooled meta-analysis, NOT Kenya-specific; no Kenya national SCD survey exists

mci.age: elderly (age band not stated in abstract)
mci.criteria: 'moderate cognitive impairment' per Russian-tradition classification (умеренные когнитивные нарушения ~ MCI) — NON-STANDARD vs Petersen; also pronounced CI 15.4%, dementia 8.2%. Preliminary conference-abstract numbers, likely selected sample; treat as very approximate
mci.citation: Abstract 338, 'Risk factors for moderate cognitive impairment and dementia in the Kazakh and Kyrgyz populations (preliminary results)', International Psychogeriatrics 2020 — need full text for exact n/age/sampling
mci.representative: community/clinic (preliminary, sampling unclear)

mci.criteria: DSM-IV + IWG (Petersen/Winblad) consensus criteria via two-stage screening + full neuropsychological diagnostic phase; clinically diagnosed, NOT MoCA-only so not over-estimated
mci.source_type: national-survey (govt Nationwide Survey on Dementia Epidemiology of Korea / National Institute of Dementia, MOHW)
mci.citation: Suh SW, Kim YJ, Kwak KP, et al. A 9-Year Comparison of Dementia Prevalence in Korea: Results of NaSDEK 2008 and 2017. J Alzheimers Dis. 2021;81(2):821-831 (PMID 33843678). Nationwide Survey on Dementia Epidemiology of Korea, representative random sample of elderly 65+, two-stage design (screening + diagnostic), MCI per IWG consensus criteria. Age/sex-standardized all-cause dementia fell 12.3%->9.8% (2008->2017). Triangulation: NaSDEK 2008 standardized MCI 24.1% (95% CI 21.0-27.2); 2016 nationwide dementia epidemiology survey (Central Dementia Center/MOHW, report NDR-1603-0015) standardized MCI 22.25% total (M 18.09%, F 25.28%), age 65+.
mci.access: paywalled(SAGE/IOS Press) - abstract OA; govt NaSDEK survey report open
mci.representative: national - randomly sampled representative community elderly aged 65+ across South Korea
mci.n: NaSDEK 2017: 2,972 screening / 474 diagnostic (65+); NaSDEK 2008: 6,141 screening / 1,673 diagnostic

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item - subjective memory complaint via KDSQ-P (Pre-screening Korean Dementia Screening Questionnaire) at national health screening; NOT SCD-I structured framework, so inflates (community estimates ranged 22.1-56.0%)
scd.year: 2020 (published; National Screening Program for Transitional Ages cohort, screening ~2007-2012, follow-up avg 6.68 yr)
scd.source_type: national-cohort (National Screening Program for Transitional Ages, age-66 nationwide screening cohort, NHIS)
scd.citation: Subjective cognitive decline and subsequent dementia: a nationwide cohort study of 579,710 people aged 66 years in South Korea. Alzheimers Res Ther. 2020;12(1):52 (PMC7203882). SCD prevalence 38.3% (222,056/579,710) defined by subjective memory impairment on KDSQ-P; comparable to prior community-based studies (22.1-56.0%). Single self-report item inflates estimate.
scd.representative: national (age-66 only; near-complete national health-screening cohort, very large n but single birth-year age band)

mci.criteria: MMSE (Arabic; MMSE-based MCI classification in a geriatric-clinic sample — note MMSE usually underestimates MCI, but this clinic-referred sample yields a high 34.7%)
mci.citation: Factors associated with cognitive function outcomes among older adults in Kuwait (geriatric outpatient unit), BMC Geriatr 2025 (DOI 10.1186/s12877-025-05882-0); 253 adults >=60, MMSE: 51.0% normal, 34.7% MCI, 14.2% moderate/severe
mci.representative: clinic (single geriatric outpatient unit; not population-representative)

mci.age: older adults (age band per full text; MCI higher with older age and lower education; median MoCA 22)
mci.criteria: MoCA screen (score <=26) THEN multidisciplinary-team clinical diagnosis (Petersen-style confirmation) — stronger than MoCA-only, but MoCA screening threshold + regional sample; 30% is high
mci.citation: Tsoy RT, et al. 2019, 'Prevalence of Mild Cognitive Impairment Among Older People in Kazakhstan and Potential Risk Factors: A Cross-sectional Study', Alzheimer Dis Assoc Disord 33(2):144-148; Almaty region; 790 eligible, 668 participated (85% response)
mci.subnational: single region (Almaty area)
mci.representative: regional/community (Almaty region cross-sectional sample, not national)

mci.criteria: HDS-R (Revised Hasegawa Dementia Scale); broad 'cognitive impairment' screen (any level), NOT DSM-5/Petersen MCI — captures dementia-range cases and overestimates; tool recorded
mci.citation: Assessment of cognitive function among adults aged >=60 years using the Revised Hasegawa Dementia Scale: cross-sectional study, Lao PDR. Health Research Policy and Systems. 2022. WHO Kobe Centre-linked national cognitive assessment. n=2206; 49.3% any cognitive impairment (men 39.7%, women 59.0%). Broad HDS-R category, not clean MCI.
mci.subnational: 6 districts across 3 provinces (rural+urban); sex- and region-stratified results reported
mci.representative: community; largest Lao cognitive survey, WHO STEPS-linked, but 3 of ~17 provinces so not fully national

mci.criteria: MCI (Petersen-type clinical/neuropsych criteria) in an urban primary-care sample
mci.citation: Mild Cognitive Impairment and modifiable risk factors among Lebanese older adults in primary care (Beirut tertiary-center clinic), Asian J Psychiatr 2021; 337 adults >=60, MCI 14.8% (abstract carries the value)
mci.representative: clinic (urban primary-care clinic, Beirut; not population-representative)

scd.criteria: self-report-single-item (fair/poor self-rated subjective memory) — single-item measure INFLATES SCD; not the structured SCD-I framework
scd.citation: Subjective Memory and its Association with Cognitive Performance among Older Adults: findings from the Lebanon Study on Aging and HeAlth (LSAHA) population-based cohort; 56.3% reported fair/poor subjective memory among n=1245 responders (of 1502 adults >=60, rural district, recruited 2023)
scd.representative: community (LSAHA population-based cohort, single rural district)

mci.criteria: MoCA (Tamil Sri Lanka validated version; Coonghe). TRAP: MoCA overestimates MCI (~20%+) — the 20.9% figure is consistent with this upward bias; not Petersen/DSM-5 criteria
mci.citation: Coonghe PAD (2015), MD thesis, 'Prevalence of mild cognitive impairment and its selected correlates among elders in Jaffna district'. MCI prevalence 20.9% (95% CI 18.4-23.4). Unpublished thesis data cited in: 'Dementia Research Activity in Sri Lanka: A Review', J Neurodegener Disord 2020;3:015 (scholars.direct). Need full thesis text for exact n and precise MoCA cut-off.
